DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF AMITABHO CHATTOPADHYAY'S MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED TRUTH OF FACTS AND GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AS TO DEFENDANT VICTORIA GALEA. THE COURT MODIFIES ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE BECAUSE THE DECLARATIONS ATTACHED TO THE MOTIONS SHOW THAT THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS AT ISSUE WERE ONLY SERVED BY EMAIL BY THE PLAINTIFF IN THE ACTION (WHO IS REPRESENTED IN PROPRIA PERSONA). CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE (CCP) SECTION 2016.050 PROVIDES THAT CCP SECTION 1013 APPLIES TO SERVICE OF DISCOVERY; CCP SECTION 1013 IN TURN REFERENCES CCP 1010.6, FOR ELECTRONIC SERVICE. THESE RULES PROVIDE THAT SERVICE BY EMAIL IS ONLY ALLOWED IF A PARTY HAS AGREED TO ACCEPT SUCH SERVICE. THE RECORD DOES NOT SHOW THAT THESE DEFENDANTS AGREED TO ACCEPT SERVICE OF DISCOVERY BY EMAIL. THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: J. TIMOTHY NARDELL, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been continued from Monday, December 17, 2018 for further Prove-Up Hearing/Short Cause Trial.
Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6048. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Plaintiff Amitabho Chattopadhyay was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Michael Rowell or Lucia Lee Parker.
Plaintiff's second amended complaint was filed on March 27, 2017.
Defendant MIchael Rowell's answer to plaintiff's second amended complaint was filed on May 2, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant Lucia Lee Parker on September 25, 2017.
Stipulation & Order determining liability as to defendant Michael Rowell was filed on December 28, 2017. This Order set damages for said defendant at $100,000.00.
Notice of Time & Place of Trial was served on December 6, 2017, setting trial for December 17, 2018.
Dismissal with prejudice as to defendant Victoria Galea was filed on September 11, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Amitabho Chattopadhyay, and against defendants Michael Rowell and Lucia Lee Parker, as follows:

Damages Attributed to Defendant Michael Rowell:
Violations of San Francisco Adminstrative Code Section 37.10B - $5,000.00.
Violations of Civil Code Section 789.3 - $10,000.00.
Compensatory Damages (Pain & Suffering) - $10,000.00.
Physicial Injuries - $20,000.00.
Emotional Distress - $20,000.00.
Punitive Damages - $35,000.00.

Total - $100,000.00.

Damages Attributed to Defendant Lucia Lee Parker:
Violations of SF Admistrative Code Section 37.10B(c)(5) - $60,000.00; Trebled to $180,000.00.
Punitive Damages - $40,000.00.

Total - $220,000.00.

Plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victoria Galea To Fifth And Sixth Set Of Requests For Production And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Amitabho Chattopadhyay, Tel. 415.481.7495, plaintiff / moving party;
Stephanie Davin, Esq. of Rankin Stock Heaberlin, Tel. 408.293.0463, appeared for defendant / opposing party Victoria Galea.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by Judge Pro Tem David Helbraun.

Matter called, argument held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling Granting Plaintiff Amitabho Chattopadhyay's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victoria Galea To Fifth And Sixth Set Of Requests For Production And For Monetary Sanctions as follows:

Plaintiff moves to compel further written responses to document requests Set 5, Request 1 and Set 6, Requests 1-5. Set 5, Request 1 (copies of Rent Board decisions): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled.
Set 6, Request 1 (valuation of rental property): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled.
Set 6, Request 2 (consultants' communications or documents concerning the rental property): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled.
Set 6, Request 3 (budgets and revenue documents and forecasts): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled.
Set 6, Request 4 (communications and other documents from lenders, partners, etc. concerning the rental property): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled.
Set 6, Request 5 (communications and other documents regarding long-term business strategy relating to the rental property): Good cause exists to grant the motion. Defendant's objections are overruled.
Request for Punitive Damages: No substantial justification has been offered by defendant for her meritless objections or her opposition to this motion. The failure to deliver any responsive documents whatsoever - such as the Rent Board decisions - and thereby limit the scope of this motion appears to be an obstruction of the discovery process. Punitive damages in the amount $711 is granted to plaintiff against defendant's attorneys and defendant, for plaintiff's cited expenses.

Order signed as modified in open court. The Court set all compliance dates at 14 days from the date of the order. (See order filed this date for the Court's ruling/modifications.)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant Victoria Galea And Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration
Off Calendar Apr-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF AMITABHO CHATTOPADHYAY'S MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED TRUTH OF FACTS AND GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AS TO DEFENDANT VICTORIA GALEA

Appearances: Amitabho Chattopadhyay (415.481.7495) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Plaintiff stipulates to the Judge Pro Tem hearing the matter, and clarifies that the motion as to defendant Michael Rowell was taken off calendar. The motion as to defendant Victoria Galea is argued. The Court modifies its ruling as follows: denied without prejudice because the declarations attached to the motions show that the discovery requests at issue were only served by email by the plaintiff in the action (who is represented in propria persona). Code of Civil Procedure (CCP) section 2016.050 provides that CCP section 1013 applies to service of discovery; CCP section 1013 in turn references CCP 1010.6, for electronic service. These rules provide that service by email is only allowed if a party has agreed to accept such service. The record does not show that these defendants agreed to accept service of discovery by email. The Court will issue an order. Judge Pro Tem: J. Timothy Nardell, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
